'''Chenachochan''' was a riverside [[City/Legends|city]] on [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]].

==Description==
Chenachochan was a [[City/Legends|city]] on the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. In the time after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], the city was near three [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Jammer/Legends|jammers]], and Chenachochan itself had been taken over by the Empire and [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshan]] [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]]. The city itself featured a [[command center]], [[prefab shelter]]s, and military buildings such as a [[Barracks|troop center]].<ref name="SW:GB" />

==History==
In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], shortly after [[Declaration of a New Order|the birth]] of the Galactic Empire, Chenachochan was attacked during the [[Battle of Kashyyyk (19 BBY)|Imperial occupation of Kashyyyk]].<ref name="DV">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]''</ref>

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], after escaping the city of [[Okikuti]] and the Wookiee [[Weebacca]]'s trap, [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] members [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], the latter of whom was a Wookiee himself, reached Chenachochan and met with [[Shoran]]. From there, they recruited a Wookiee [[Army/Legends|army]] to destroy the three Imperial jammers in order to communicate with the New Republic about the situation on Kashyyyk. The city was subsequently infiltrated by the Wookiee army and freed, and became a staging area for the [[Liberation of Kashyyyk/Legends|assault]] on [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Hindane Darcc/Legends|Darcc's]] [[Darcc's Palace|palace]].<ref name="SW:GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Chenachochan was first mentioned in the [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|strategy guide]] for the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. It appeared and was first identified during two Chewbacca campaign missions. The missions and thus the city was also included in the game's [[2002]] expansion pack, [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']]. In the second mission, Trandoshan and Imperial buildings are spread throughout the map and hold captured Wookiee units. The player has the option of freeing each of those units. In both missions the size of the army is the player's choice.
